area_anchor: Brikette — A guest could receive a stale or wrong email if staff edit and send at the same time
current_truth: The draft send route reads the current draft and sends it without verifying it hasn't changed since the staff member last saw it. JSON.parse of recipient data is unprotected and can crash the send flow. Multiple draft routes use unsafe as-casts on parsed JSON.
next_scope_now: Add optimistic locking (updated_at check) before send, wrap all JSON.parse calls in safe parse utilities with error handling, replace as-casts with validated parsing.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: race condition on send — no draft version check; unprotected JSON.parse crashes send; unsafe type casts in draft routes
why: If two people work on the same email draft, or if the system updates a draft while staff are about to send it, the guest could receive an outdated or wrong response. Additionally, if stored recipient data is corrupted, the send flow crashes silently and the email never goes out. Staff have no way to know this happened.

intended_outcome.statement: Draft send verifies the draft hasn't changed since last viewed, and all JSON parsing in draft routes is safe with proper error handling.
